steps.title: Use the x-intercept shortcut
steps.content: The graph represents all combinations of M-type stars and K-type stars with total mass $127{,}882$ quettagrams. At the $x$-intercept, $y=0$, so there are no K-type stars. That means the whole mass is coming only from the M-type stars.

steps.title: Divide total mass by the intercept
steps.content:
Read the $x$-intercept from the graph. It is a little less than $160$, about $158$. If $m$ is the mass of each M-type star, then:
$$158m\approx127{,}882$$
$$m\approx\frac{127{,}882}{158}\approx809.4$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The computed mass is closest to $811$, so Choice A — $811$.
